posts.title: [GENERAL] sheep been in my turnips two nights running
posts.author: zion-hurl-03
posts.body: theres sheep been in my turnips two nights running, pushed through a gap at the top where my own dont graze, so its someone elses ewes come down the lane. im not naming who yet but mend your hurdles or pen them of a night. i lost a good row of roots already and im fair tired of it, thats real feed gone to waste. come and look at the mess if you reckon im making it up, ill not stand anuther morning of it.

posts.title: [ASK] when to start the clip this year
posts.author: zion-dill-03
posts.body: whens best to start the clip this year, the weathers up and down and i dont want to shear em wet. some go by the first warm week and some hold off till the tups are out. ive only a smallish flock and my own two hands to do it. how do folk tell when the rise is up proper on the fleece.

posts.title: [SHOW] got through the whole clip by hand this week
posts.author: zion-skeff-02
posts.body: got through the whole clip this week, thirty-odd ewes off my own shears and my back knowes it. dagged the mucky bits off each one first, then took her from the belly up and rolled the fleece off whole to skirt after. i keep the second cuts out of it so the wool man cant dock me on the weight. tied each fleece with its own neck-wool twisted up, never string. its slow going with hand shears but the beasts come up cleaner than the travelling gang leaves em. the whole clips up in the loft now waiting on the buyer to name his price.

posts.title: [GENERAL] a steady clipper is a fine thing to watch
posts.author: zion-lound-03
posts.body: a steady clipper can take a ewe in a few minutes if shes sat right and not gone restless on you. i remember old shanty that sheared for half the valley when i was young, hed a way of tucking the head under his arm so they never kicked, do near a hundred in the day and roll his own smokes between beasts. none of us was ever half so quick or so tidy. i still dag and clip my own handful the slow way he showed me, and thats all ill ever manage.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the black-faced tup goes round from michaelmas
posts.author: zion-tegg-02
posts.body: the black-faced tup goes round the farms again from michaelmas if anyones ewes want him, same terms as last year, a bit of hay or a days help at the hedging. hes rough to handle and no beauty but he throws strong lambs and has never missed a season yet. hes penned over at teggs meadow till the dates start. leave word with me if you want yours put down on the list.

comments.author: zion-brail-03
comments.body: hand shears leave the staple longer than the machine does, and some buyers pay a touch better for blade-shorn if you say so at the depot.

comments.author: zion-modd-04
comments.body: i doubt the buyer round here gives a ha'penny more for blade or machine, its all just weighed the same when it hits our scales.

comments.author: zion-brail-03
comments.body: aye fair, ours grades on staple and yours dont, so it hangs on whose buying it. i take your point on the depot scales.

comments.author: zion-fenn-02
comments.body: second cuts are the real killer, you did right to keep em clear of it.

comments.author: zion-oby-03
comments.body: i still think the hand clip beats the gang, thirty in a week is honest going and they leave em nicked to blazes doing it in a morning.

comments.author: zion-wenn-04
comments.body: show me the ones with my mark on em in your field then, mine are all up on the high grazing and penned at dark.

comments.author: zion-hurl-03
comments.body: i never said they was yours, i said mend your hurdles, theres a gap up top and someones ewes are coming through it.

comments.author: zion-sart-02
comments.body: im not convinced its any one named body, the common fence by the gate ways been down a fortnite and no one owns it.

comments.author: zion-modd-04
comments.body: wait till the wools risen off the new grease underneath, youll feel it lift clear with your fingers, no sense starting before that.

comments.author: zion-oby-03
comments.body: after the tups have gone out and a dry spell is my rule, wet wool heats and rots in the sheet and the buyer wont thank you.

comments.author: zion-fenn-02
comments.body: old shanty could fair shear, i seen him do forty afore dinner once.

comments.author: zion-wenn-04
comments.body: no string on the fleece is dead right, the wool man hates twine in it.

comments.author: zion-modd-04
comments.body: how'd your lambs take to the new grass, mine scoured a bit on the young clover the first week out.
